channel.title: Tutor — Weekly Wrist Arthroscopy
channel.description: A weekly 20–30 minute two-host walk through the past week of wrist ARTHROSCOPY literature — arthroscopic techniques for scaphoid nonunion, scapholunate & lunotriquetral ligament injuries, TFCC tears, DRUJ instability, distal radius fractures/malunions, midcarpal instability, CMC/wrist arthritis, SLAC/SNAC, Kienböck, and cartilage injuries. Key studies, corresponding authors, and clinically relevant takeaways. Generated automatically from PubMed.
channel.itunes:author: Eric R. Wagner, MD, MSc
channel.itunes:summary: A weekly 20–30 minute two-host walk through the past week of wrist ARTHROSCOPY literature — arthroscopic techniques for scaphoid nonunion, scapholunate & lunotriquetral ligament injuries, TFCC tears, DRUJ instability, distal radius fractures/malunions, midcarpal instability, CMC/wrist arthritis, SLAC/SNAC, Kienböck, and cartilage injuries. Key studies, corresponding authors, and clinically relevant takeaways. Generated automatically from PubMed.

item.title: Wrist Arthroscopy — 2026-07-19
item.itunes:author: Eric R. Wagner, MD, MSc
item.description: Weekly Wrist Arthroscopy Literature Digest — 2026-07-19 Pulled from PubMed across the six target journals — JBJS Am, J Hand Surg Am, Plast Reconstr Surg, J Wrist Surg, J Hand Surg Eur Vol, and Hand Clinics. Restricted to articles focused on wrist arthroscopic techniques. Author line = first & senior/corresponding author; corresponding-author institution listed per article. No new qualifying articles this week. A 7-day and 30-day PubMed entry-date search across all six journals turned up nothing newly indexed. The window was widened to the full prior 90 days (the same check performed in the 2026-07-04 and 2026-07-11 runs) and cross-referenced against all three prior digests (2026-07-03, 2026-07-04, 2026-07-11). Of the four candidate PMIDs in that 90-day pool, all four were already accounted for: - Modified Viegas capsuloplasty for chronic SL Geissler 3C lesions (J Hand Surg Eur Vol, PMID 42246463) — already featured 2026-07-03. - All-arthroscopic anatomical repair of complete foveal TFCC tears (J Wrist Surg, PMID 42179684) — already featured 2026-07-03. - Surface alterations/chondrocyte activity after monopolar RF treatment (J Hand Surg Eur Vol, PMID 42093184) — already featured 2026-07-03. - Bilateral dynamic CT for scapholunate instability (Plast Reconstr Surg, PMID 42059558) — re-checked and again excluded: it is a diagnostic-imaging protocol study; arthroscopy appears only as the reference-standard confirmation for cohort selection, not as the technique under study (same exclusion made in the 2026-07-04 digest). This is the fourth consecutive week without new material in this niche literature — J Bone Joint Surg Am, J Hand Surg Am, Plast Reconstr Surg, J Wrist Surg, J Hand Surg Eur Vol, and Hand Clinics all had zero newly-qualifying wrist-arthroscopy citations. Because there is nothing new to report, this week's podcast episode is a thematic round-up of the 24 wrist-arthroscopy articles surfaced across the program so far (2026-07-03 through 2026-07-11), grouped by clinical theme rather than by journal — see Episodes/2026-07-19 Tutor Wrist.txt. No new author-outreach drafts were created this week (see Outreach/2026-07-19 Outreach.md). --- Generated automatically from PubMed. This track runs weekly over the prior 7 days, widening the window when the literature is thin, as it has been for four consecutive weeks now.

item.title: Wrist Arthroscopy — 2026-07-11
item.itunes:author: Eric R. Wagner, MD, MSc
item.description: Weekly Wrist Arthroscopy Literature Digest — 2026-07-11 Pulled from PubMed across the six target journals — JBJS Am, J Hand Surg Am, Plast Reconstr Surg, J Wrist Surg, J Hand Surg Eur Vol, and Hand Clinics. Restricted to articles focused on wrist arthroscopic techniques. Author line = first & senior/corresponding author; corresponding-author institution listed per article. Sweeper/catch-up run (the normal Saturday run did not fire this week). A narrow 7-day and 14-day PubMed entry-date search across all six journals again turned up no newly indexed wrist-arthroscopy citations — the same pattern seen in the 2026-07-04 run, so this appears to be a genuine lull in this niche rather than a search error. The window was widened and checked against the full topic-term list (scaphoid, scapholunate, lunotriquetral, TFCC, DRUJ, Kienböck, midcarpal, SLAC/SNAC) across all six journals, then cross-referenced against the two prior digests (2026-07-03, 2026-07-04) to exclude anything already covered. 4 genuinely new qualifying articles were found — all in journals/topics not previously captured. JBJS Am, J Hand Surg Am, and Hand Clinics had no new qualifying arthroscopic-technique wrist articles this week beyond what was already featured. --- Journal of Wrist Surgery (1) Short-Term Follow-Up Treatment of Thumb Carpometacarpal Joint Osteoarthritis Through Total Arthroscopic Trapeziectomy with Suture-Button Suspensionplasty Martin Lozoya J & Larrainzar-Garijo R — Hospital Universitario Infanta Leonor / Hospital Universitario Quironsalud Madrid, Madrid, Spain A single-center cohort of 42 patients with severe (Eaton III–IV) thumb CMC osteoarthritis treated with total arthroscopic trapeziectomy plus suture-button suspensionplasty, reporting significant DASH and pain improvement at over 1 year and high patient satisfaction (4.5/5). PubMed --- Journal of Hand Surgery (European Volume) (2) Arthroscopic-assisted excision of avascular osteochondral fragments of the proximal pole of the scaphoid Rigo IZ — Østfold Hospital Trust, Norway A two-case report describing arthroscopic excision of small avascular osteochondral fragments in proximal scaphoid nonunions with an intact scapholunate ligament, proposed as a way to treat select proximal-pole nonunions without destabilizing the wrist. PubMed The evolving role of wrist arthroscopy Del Piñal F — private practice, Spain A review/commentary examining four recent arthroscopy papers — ulnar portal "nerve density" mapping, closed TFCC repair, arthroscopic scaphoid nonunion treatment, and arthroscopic perilunate injury management — arguing that better training and bone-ligament healing research are the next frontiers for wrist arthroscopy. PubMed --- Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery (1) Evaluating the Optimal Treatment Pathway for Volar Wrist Ganglia: A Cost-Minimization Analysis Rogers MJ & Kazmers NH — University of Utah, Salt Lake City, UT, USA A decision-analytic economic model comparing aspiration, open excision, and ar

item.title: Wrist Arthroscopy — 2026-07-04
item.itunes:author: Eric R. Wagner, MD, MSc
item.description: Weekly Wrist Arthroscopy Literature Digest — 2026-07-04 Pulled from PubMed across the six target journals — JBJS Am, J Hand Surg Am, Plast Reconstr Surg, J Wrist Surg, J Hand Surg Eur Vol, and Hand Clinics. Restricted to articles focused on wrist arthroscopic techniques. Author line = first & senior/corresponding author; corresponding-author institution listed per article. A quiet week. A 7-day and even a 14-day PubMed entry-date search across the six journals turned up no newly indexed wrist-arthroscopy articles, so the window was widened to the prior 90 days by publication date. Of the ~21 arthroscopic-technique citations in that wider window, 19 were already covered in last week's inaugural digest (2026-07-03) — only 2 articles are genuinely new since then, both in the Journal of Hand Surgery (American Volume). JBJS Am, Plastic & Reconstructive Surgery, J Wrist Surg, J Hand Surg Eur Vol, and Hand Clinics had no new qualifying articles this week. (One additional 90-day hit, a bilateral dynamic-CT protocol paper for scapholunate instability out of Radboud/Plast Reconstr Surg, was excluded as a diagnostic-imaging study with no arthroscopic technique component — arthroscopy is used there only as a reference-standard confirmation, not the focus of the paper.) --- Journal of Hand Surgery (American Volume) (2) Interobserver Reliability of the Trampoline Test, Hook Test, and Suction Test in Arthroscopic Assessment of Triangular Fibrocartilage Complex Lesions Hasegawa H & De Villeneuve Bargemon JB — La Timone Adult Hospital, Aix Marseille University, Marseille, France A blinded review of 21 wrist arthroscopy videos by three board-certified hand surgeons testing agreement on three intraoperative TFCC diagnostic signs, finding the hook test most reproducible (excellent interobserver agreement) while the trampoline and suction tests showed only moderate reliability. PubMed Effect of Radial Lengthening on the Stability of the Distal Radioulnar Joint: A Biomechanical Cadaveric Study Yin CY & Kakar S — Mayo Clinic, Rochester, MN, USA A cadaveric biomechanical study (n=8 specimens) using an arthroscopically created TFCC injury model to test whether radial lengthening (1, 3, 5 mm) restores DRUJ stability, finding no consistent benefit from lengthening alone and cautioning against relying on it to treat DRUJ instability after distal radius fracture. PubMed --- Generated automatically from PubMed. This track runs weekly over the prior 7 days, widening the window when the literature is thin, as it was this week.

item.title: Wrist Arthroscopy — 2026-07-03
item.itunes:author: Eric R. Wagner, MD, MSc
item.description: Weekly Wrist Arthroscopy Literature Digest — 2026-07-03 Pulled from PubMed across the six target journals — JBJS Am, J Hand Surg Am, Plast Reconstr Surg, J Wrist Surg, J Hand Surg Eur Vol, and Hand Clinics. Restricted to articles focused on wrist arthroscopic techniques (arthroscopic treatment of scaphoid nonunion, scapholunate/lunotriquetral injuries, TFCC tears, DRUJ instability, distal radius fractures/malunions, midcarpal instability, CMC/wrist arthritis, SLAC/SNAC, Kienböck, cartilage injuries, etc.). Author line = first & senior author; corresponding-author institution listed per article. 18 articles (this first run widened to a ~18-month window for a fuller sample; going forward this runs weekly over the prior 7 days). JBJS Am and Plastic & Reconstructive Surgery had no qualifying arthroscopic-technique wrist articles in this window. --- Hand Clinics (7) A themed issue on open-versus-arthroscopic wrist surgery. Wrist Anatomy: Open and Arthroscopic Surgical Approaches Emanuels AF & Wu KY — Mayo Clinic, Rochester, MN, USA A practical review of volar and dorsal carpal anatomy as seen through open and arthroscopic approaches, framing how the choice of approach in the young arthritic wrist is dictated by the pathology and the exposure needed to work safely. PubMed Arthroscopic Treatment of Thumb Carpometacarpal Joint Arthritis Bougioukli S & Chung KC — University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, MI, USA A review of arthroscopic-assisted management of basilar thumb arthritis, arguing that wrist-arthroscopy techniques address several shortcomings of open trapeziectomy while the optimal procedure remains unsettled. PubMed Total Wrist Arthrodesis: Open Versus Arthroscopic Alsayed A & Awan H — The Ohio State University, Columbus, OH, USA A review of indications and techniques for total wrist fusion in advanced rheumatoid or post-traumatic arthritis (including SLAC/SNAC), comparing open and arthroscopic approaches and stressing careful patient selection given the trade of motion for pain relief. PubMed Proximal Row Carpectomy: Open Versus Arthroscopy Lim RQR & Chim H — Louisiana State University Health Sciences Center, New Orleans, LA, USA A review of arthroscopic proximal row carpectomy as a motion-preserving salvage option, weighing the promise of less soft-tissue injury and faster rehab against a real learning curve and longer operative time. PubMed Atypical Fusions Radioscapholunate/Lunotriquetral: Open vs Arthroscopic Tse S & Bayne CO — University of California, Davis, Sacramento, CA, USA A review of RSL and LT limited fusions for arthritis/instability, noting that distal scaphoid and triquetral excision can improve motion and that arthroscopic approaches improve visualization at the cost of a steep learning curve. PubMed Current Trends in 4-Corner and 3-Corner Fusions: Comparing Open and Arthroscopic Approaches Paul R & Persitz J — Toronto Western Hospital, University Health Network, Toronto, Canada A review of 4- and 3-corner fusions for SLAC/SNAC art
